content:
I know of two other jugglers in this group, perhaps only one of whom is on Zulip. I just heard about this:

www.jugglingedge.com/event.php?EventID=6127
Informal juggling event this Saturday 10:00-19:00 at Cambridge Common
I have other plans though.
Wow, nice, but I'll be in NH (Santa's Village).
An event tomorrow (Monday): A Juggler's Guide to Many-Body Quantum Mechanics - https://cambridgepl.libcal.com/event/13067755
Looks cool, don't think I'd want to make the trip for that tho.

https://youtube.com/watch?v=NGaMUeAfRJI

The most balls ever juggled is now 12.
Qualifying means 2 catches for every ball. This barely qualifies.
Amazing. @Ned Batchelder check it out. ^^
That is impressive!

BJF: BOSTON JUGGLING AND FLOW
Come to the BJF celebration festival!

The MIT Juggling Club will be 50 years old, and Boston SpinJam will be 20 years old!

When: 9:30AM to 10:30PM, Saturday April 4, 2026. No cost, completely free admission.

Where: Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Morss Hall, Walker Memorial building #50, 142 Memorial Drive, Cambridge, MA 02139.

What: A day of Juggling and Flow arts! Including games, informal workshops, and free treats.

Why: The MIT juggling club was started 50 years ago by Arthur Lewbel in 1976. It's now the oldest juggling club in continuous operation in the entire world! Plus, the MIT Spinning Arts club is 20 years old!

https://mitjuggling.club/
13 hours of juggling? :smile:
I'm putting it in my calendar, at least. Thanks!
Some of these conventions last days.
It involves a lot of socializing and hanging out and shows and competitions.
Workshops, buying and selling equipment.

Coming up next weekend! A better site for it:

https://mitjuggling.club/bjf/index.html
Nice. I'll try to make it.
@Dan still going? what time? I was thinking morning.
late morning, that is :sweat_smile:
um, or early afternoon... I got sucked into vibe coding: #boston > Codex
hmm, my daughter called and is coming home. I might go later, if at all :sweat_smile:
Hey sorry I'm here now. I'll be here for the remainder. Not a huge room so you should be able to find me. I'll introduce you to my NH crew! If you can make it.
I'll step away for dinner though, again with the NH crew.

Are you going to be there basically all day? I just go my daughter home (crazy traffic, due to a shooting at Northeastern, a 3 minute walk from her place). I'm hoping I can stop by later, like dinner time or whatever.
Yeah. So there's a show (also free) at 7:30 in a different hall, then around 9:00 when that's over I'll head out. But the festival wraps up around 10.
But right before the show I'll be out for dinner leaving around 5:30.
Cool, cool. I might just text you once I know if I'm even coming. Enjoy! :juggling:
